claim: Fully duplex speech-to-speech systems (Moshi) using neural audio codecs bypass traditional ASR/TTS bottlenecks, enabling <200ms real-time latency
actionable: For real-time conversational agents, explore audio codec approaches (MIMI) over traditional ASR/LLM/TTS pipelines to reduce latency

claim: Audio tokenization with semantic and acoustic information allows multi-stream modeling to handle overlapping speech without explicit end-of-speech detection
actionable: Consider audio tokenization approaches for agent frontends to enable natural interruption-handling and overlapping speech in conversations

claim: Generative models can bootstrap their own training data at scale (20,000 hours synthesized); use LLM-generated scripts for voice model training
actionable: For voice AI projects, use language models to generate diverse training scripts with variations (accents, conditions) before synthesizing training audio

claim: Open-source speech systems with permissive licenses (MIT, Apache 2.0) enable component-level reuse; separate codec, language, and voice models
actionable: License voice AI components separately and permissively to let users swap codecs, language models, and voices independently
